intended_use: The Venue is a general purpose diagnostic ultrasound system for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals for ultrasound imaging, measurement, display and analysis of the human body and fl
intended_use_full: The Venue is a general purpose diagnostic ultrasound system for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals for ultrasound imaging, measurement, display and analysis of the human body and fluid. Venue is intended to be used in a hospital or medical clinic. Venue clinical applications include: abdominal (GYN and Urology), thoracic/pleural, ophthalmic, Fetal/OB, Small Organ (including breast, testes, thyroid), Vascular/Peripheral vascular, neonatal and adult cephalic, pediatric, musculoskeletal (conventional and superficial), cardiac (adults and pediatric), Transrectal, Transvaginal, Transesophageal, Intraoperative (vascular) and interventional guidance (includes tissue biopsy, fluid drainage, vascular and non-vascular access).
target_condition: Nerve block procedures requiring nerve localization and tracking
ground_truth: Ground truth annotations of nerve bundles were obtained by a single clinical expert marking the anatomical area of nerves in each frame of scouting sequences.
other_metrics: Sequence accuracy requirement: at least 70% of sequences are meaningfully detected, and at least 80% of meaningfully detected sequences are successfully detected (meeting frame accuracy criteria). Frame accuracy was verified across demographic subgroups (Gender M/F, Age <60/>=60, BMI <=25/>25) and all supported nerve block locations and probe types.
